label: Penalty Fares and Fare Evasion Policy – All Networks

penalty_fare.definition: A penalty fare is a charge issued to a passenger who is found travelling without a valid ticket, or who has not tapped in/out correctly at the relevant gate or barrier.

conditions: Failing to tap in at the origin gate
Failing to tap out at the destination gate
Using an expired or invalid ticket
metro.issued_by: Authorised ticket inspection staff or automated gate systems.

conditions: Travelling on a different service or class than booked without a valid change
Using an expired or invalid ticket
national_rail.issued_by: Authorised ticket inspection staff or conductors.

fare_evasion.definition: Fare evasion is the deliberate act of travelling without paying the applicable fare, or of using fraudulent means to obtain a discounted or free ticket.
examples: Presenting a counterfeit or altered ticket
Using a child fare ticket when the passenger is aged 16 or over
Claiming a group or discount fare without meeting the eligibility criteria
Tailgating through barriers without tapping in

consequences.immediate: The passenger may be required to purchase a full-price ticket immediately or be removed from the service.
consequences.penalty_fare: A penalty fare will be issued in addition to the outstanding fare.
consequences.prosecution: In cases of deliberate or repeated fare evasion, the operator reserves the right to refer the matter to law enforcement. Prosecution may result in a fine of up to $500.00 and/or a travel ban.
consequences.travel_ban: Passengers found to have committed serious or repeated fare evasion may be banned from using the network for a period determined by the operator.

appeals.how_to_appeal: Submit a written appeal via the app, email, or by post within 14 days of the penalty fare being issued, quoting the penalty reference number.
grounds_for_appeal: Ticket was purchased but could not be presented due to a technical fault
Medical or emergency circumstances prevented normal ticketing
appeals.escalation: If an appeal is rejected and the passenger disagrees with the decision, they may escalate to the independent transport adjudicator within 28 days.

unpaid_penalty_fares.reminder_process: A reminder notice will be sent after 21 days if payment has not been received.
unpaid_penalty_fares.debt_recovery: Unpaid penalty fares may be referred to a debt collection agency after 42 days. Additional administration charges may apply.
unpaid_penalty_fares.legal_action: The operator reserves the right to pursue unpaid penalty fares through civil court proceedings.

staff_powers.inspection: Authorised inspection staff may request to see a valid ticket and photo ID from any passenger at any time.
staff_powers.refusal_to_comply: Passengers who refuse to show a valid ticket or ID, or who behave aggressively toward staff, may be removed from the service. Law enforcement may be called if necessary.
